element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player selects scenario and family at outset, then begins with available units for initial territory control
Turn-based tactics battle screen where player moves units about the map; demonstrated through initial adjacent territory attacks
Certain units may build fences to keep enemies out; learned through battle necessity

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After initial battles, through unit roster expansion
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player may field up to five units including wizard or hired unit; more powerful options unlocked as game progresses
Player can upgrade territory economy or defenses as one of available monthly actions
Player may propose alliances or plunder adjacent provinces; strategic tool for expansion beyond direct conquest
Dragons available as powerful units; thematically tied to Fire Dragon and Pastha lore

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Narrative backdrop; mechanical through family selection
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Six wizard gems distributed among families (Zendor/Lightning, Pluvius/Meteors, Scylla, Empyron, Chylla, Skulryk); passive bonuses tied to family selection

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based grand strategy with episodic tension peaks during battles; player-controlled pacing on tactics screen (one action per in-game month per territory) alternates with faster-paced turn-based tactics battles

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Non-adjacent territories cannot be attacked; forces linear expansion or diplomatic maneuvering to control non-contiguous regions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Player begins with limited territories and units; must manage single action per territory per turn strategically; learning combat system and unit capabilities
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Multiple family factions compete; player must balance territorial expansion, economy/defense upgrades, diplomacy, and direct military engagement; complexity of simultaneous threats increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Map unification objective requires conquest of entire island; endgame involves managing large resource pool, coordinating multi-territory attacks, and eliminating rival families
notable_spikes: First combat encounter - unfamiliar turn-based tactics system
Late-game coordination of large-scale invasions requiring food management and unit composition optimization

notable_level_patterns.description: Four scenarios share same game board (Isle of Ishmeria) but differ in family composition, initial provinces, vassals, and gem distribution; changes starting conditions and strategic viability
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All scenarios use same map but present different power distributions

notable_level_patterns.description: Game board divided into provinces; player controls one action per territory per turn; adjacency determines attack possibilities; creates branching strategic paths through map
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tactics screen shows right-side world map with territory control visualization

notable_level_patterns.description: Combat shifts to dedicated battle screen with turn-based tactics; player fields up to five units (4 standard types + 1 wizard/hired); separate from territory acquisition layer
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Battle screen encounters when attacking or defending adjacent territories
